mission.title: metallb: Get rid of leader election for layer-2 mode
mission.description: Get rid of leader election for layer-2 mode. Requested by 7+ users.

steps.title: Check current metallb deployment
steps.description:
Verify your metallb version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n metallb-system -l app.kubernetes.io/name=metallb
helm list -n metallb-system 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working metallb installation.

steps.title: Review metallb configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant metallb configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n metallb-system -l app.kubernetes.io/name=metallb
kubectl get configmap -n metallb-system -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=metallb
```
Layer 2 modes (ARP and NDP) requires a single owner machine for each service IP. Currently we do this by running master election and having the winner own all IPs.

steps.title: Apply the fix for Get rid of leader election for layer-2 mode
steps.description:

This sgtm
(also explains why I see 4 qps of config traffic w/ leader election)

I also agree with allowing a split brain situation, instead of doing something 
complex.

--
Miek Gieben

See the source issue for community-verified solutions.

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n metallb-system -l app.kubernetes.io/name=metallb
kubectl get events -n metallb-system --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "Get rid of leader election for layer-2 mode" is functioning correctly.

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with metallb installed or the issue environment reproducible.
